description: JTI classType shape definitions. Derived from WSDL `DocumentFilingMetaDataItem` (19 fields) + catalog §3.0-§3.20 wire evidence. Authoritative for UI field rendering, builder xmlPath dispatch, and parser reverse-mapping. Open-string contract: consumers MUST fail-closed on classType values not in this file, per §3.0 observation #3.
wsdlRef: src/EFiling/EFiling.WsdlGenerated/FilingReview/Reference.cs:11397 (DocumentFilingMetaDataItem). 19 classType fields occupy XmlElement Order 1-10 and 12-20; Order=11 is `idReferences` (shared existing-data wrapper, NOT a classType).

evidenceLevels.V0: Evidence-backed, baseline-sample-verified, Tier B live-green on Madera. Production-ready.
evidenceLevels.V1: Evidence-backed (baseline samples), Tier A green, Tier B partial. Production-ready with known gaps.
evidenceLevels.V2: Layer A (JTI HTML docs) only OR Layer B (per-court variant outside baseline). Not baseline-observed. Fail-closed unless court policy declares it.
evidenceLevels.V3: WSDL-declared only. No Layer A/B/C evidence yet. Fail-closed until first-sample evidence promotes to V2+.

caseParticipant.description: Party on the case (person or organization). Most-used classType — gates 26/26 baseline SF scenarios.

wire.niemGrounding: ECF 4.0 CaseParticipantType extended to CaseParticipantExtType; wraps nc:EntityPerson / nc:EntityOrganization + ContactInformation + CaseParticipantRoleCode

existingData.notes: References an existing party on the case by primaryId (from GetCase response).

newData.wire: <caseParticipantValue> with full nc:EntityPerson / nc:EntityOrganization + role + contact
newData.notes: Inline new-party data. Audit H-1 confirmed the wire shape — prior builder dropped new-party names silently.

notSurfacedWsdlFields.reason: UX simplification — rarely used; per-court promotion possible if a court requests prefix support.
UX simplification — rarely needed in CA civil filings; promote per-court if Probate / Family demands it.
Derived field — composed from given+middle+surname on the server side; never a filer-entered value.
UX simplification — not a filer-facing concept; reserved for clerk annotations.

person.notSurfacedWsdlFieldsSource: WSDL-grounded against PersonNameType at @c:/Users/sevak/workspace/test/src/EFiling/EFiling.WsdlGenerated/FilingReview/Reference.cs:5475 (verified 2026-05-17 in pre-T-4 audit).

organization.auditNote: WSDL field enumeration pending against nc:EntityOrganizationType — surfaced set is baseline-sample-driven (just OrganizationName). Per-court promotion path: add OrganizationIdentification / OrganizationDoingBusinessAsName as discovered.

caseParticipant.additionalInfoTagsNote: Observed tags: E_SERVICE (self-rep), EFSP_EMAIL (with E_SERVICE), FEE_EXEMPTION, EFSP_FIRST_APPEARANCE_PAID. See catalog §4.

caseAssignment.description: Attorney assignment on a case. Gates 14/26 baseline SF scenarios (attorney add/remove).

wire.niemGrounding: JTI CaseAssignmentType (ecf:extension:CaseAssignmentType). Wraps nc:EntityPerson (attorney name + bar via PersonOtherIdentification) + nc:EntityOrganization (firm) + ContactInformation + AssignmentRole.

existingData.notes: References an existing attorney on the case by primaryId.

newData.wire: <caseAssignmentValue> with EntityPerson + bar + firm + contact + AssignmentRole
newData.notes: Audit C-1 fixed controller silent-drop of new-data caseAssignment.

attorney.auditNote: WSDL field enumeration pending against CaseAssignmentType (extends CaseParticipantExtType) at @c:/Users/sevak/workspace/test/src/EFiling/EFiling.WsdlGenerated/FilingReview/Reference.cs:17396 — surfaced set is baseline-sample-driven (bar + name + firmName). AssignmentRole + nested contact fields may surface as separate primitives.

fields.name: personNameSuffixText
fields.label: Suffix
fields.addedNote: Added 2026-05-17 (Step #12 prep). Was on caseParticipant.person variant (line 66) but missing from caseAssignment.attorney + attorney alias variants — pre-fix the T-4 attorney composite would silently drop suffix even though SF's hand-coded markup emitted `.att-suffix` and the wire model carries CaseAssignmentData.NameSuffix end-to-end (Step #8 closure).

caseAssignment.additionalInfoTagsNote: Audit C-3b/c — tags on caseAssignment existing-data / new-data branches were silently dropped pre-fix. Allowed tags TBD per court policy.

contact.description: Contact information (address + phone + email). Used for 5/26 baseline SF scenarios.

wire.niemGrounding: JTI ContactValue extension. NOT niem-core nc:ContactInformation — distinct namespace per audit C-2 Bug B fix.

existingData.notes: Contact is always inline; no idReferences shape.

newData.wire: <contactValue> with address1/city/state/zip/phone/email children in ns10 namespace

default.description: Standard contact card with mailing address + optional phone/email.
default.auditNote: WSDL field enumeration pending against JTI ContactValue extension at @c:/Users/sevak/workspace/test/src/EFiling/EFiling.WsdlGenerated/FilingReview/Reference.cs:22576 — surfaced set covers the 10 fields baseline-sample-observed. Extra fields (e.g., second telephone, fax) may exist; promote per-court evidence.
default.sourceLiveVerified: 2026-05-17 against Madera Court Policy via /api/codelist endpoint. STATE returned [] (wrong); US_STATE returned 50 entries. PHONE_TYPE returned [] (wrong); TELEPHONE_TYPE returned 5 entries. COUNTRY returned 797 bytes; ADDRESS_COUNTRY returned 4814 bytes (richer, per memory 8fc21aaf JTI EFM core requirements). ADDRESS_TYPE returned 675 bytes (correct).

fields.name: state
fields.label: State
fields.sourceCorrectionNote: Was 'STATE' until 2026-05-17. Live verification against Madera proved STATE returns empty array; US_STATE returns 50 entries. Incumbent SF view always used 'US_STATE'.

fields.name: country
fields.label: Country
fields.sourceCorrectionNote: Was 'COUNTRY' until 2026-05-17. Both names work but ADDRESS_COUNTRY is the canonical JTI name (per memory 8fc21aaf) and returns the comprehensive list (4814 vs 797 bytes on Madera).

fields.name: telephoneType
fields.label: Phone Type
fields.sourceCorrectionNote: Was 'PHONE_TYPE' until 2026-05-17. Live verification against Madera proved PHONE_TYPE returns empty array; TELEPHONE_TYPE returns 5 entries.

codeList.description: Dropdown selection from a court-declared code list (MOTION_TYPE, CAUSE_OF_ACTION, DEFERRED_PAYMENT, IS_WITH_HEARING, etc.).

evidence.layerAEvidence: JTI HTML docs: LASC IS_WITH_HEARING, IS_STIP, JUDSIGN, ACCOMMODATIONS, DEFERRED_PAYMENT

wire.niemGrounding: No NIEM analogue — pure JTI concept. Wire element wraps a simple string.

existingData.notes: Rare — code is its own identity; usually emitted as new-data.

default.description: Single-value dropdown sourced dynamically from court policy (`selectedCode` drives the choice).

codeList.additionalInfoTagsNote: None observed. The codeValue itself IS the value; tags would be redundant.

date.description: Date/datetime value. Used for Proof-of-Service dates (CIV-SUB-016/017) among others.

wire.niemGrounding: niem-core nc:DateType with nc:DateTime child (ISO 8601 datetime, optional timezone). Audit D-1 confirmed <DateTime> not <Date>.

existingData.notes: Dates are always inline scalar values.

default.description: Date-only or datetime; timezone optional per court configuration.

openQuestions: Timezone offset handling — baseline wire uses `-08:00`; builder currently emits without offset. Deferred pending timezone-configuration story.

boolean.description: Yes/No flag. Used for Self-Rep conversion (CIV-SUB-018) and custody flag (FAM-SUB-004).

wire.niemGrounding: xs:boolean scalar. Baseline wire uses literal `true`/`false`, NOT digit form `1`/`0` (distinct from tagValue encoding — see tag schema for `E_SERVICE` which uses `0`/`1`).

boolean.labelGroundingNote: Field label is 'Yes' (not 'Value') because boolean classType is always rendered as a yes/no checkbox where the question lives in the document-metadata's `name` (meta.Name) — e.g., 'Is the petitioner self-represented?' — and the checkbox label is the affirmative answer. Verified against incumbent SF view's inline checkbox UX 2026-05-17 + grounded in 2 baseline samples (CIV-SUB-018, FAM-SUB-004) where the meta.Name carries the question.

text.description: Free-text metadata (e.g., LASC JCCP Number per JTI HTML docs). Not observed in 48 baseline samples.

evidence.layerAEvidence: JTI HTML: Court Specific Concepts / JCCP Number Sample

existingData.notes: Uses shared idReferences when value must come from a pre-defined set (e.g., codelist-backed text).

text.promotionCriteria: First Layer B (per-court variant XML) or Layer C (Madera GetPolicy) sighting of classType=text with actual field constraints.

crsReceiptNumber.description: Calendar Reservation System (CRS) receipt number. Per JTI Document Metadata HTML doc 'Class Types' section: 'String. The calendar reservation number generated by the CRS system.' This is one of the 10 JTI canonical classTypes (alongside attorney, boolean, caseParticipant, contact, currency, date, judgment, text, codelist). Builder arm + parser arm landed Step #46 based on JTI HTML Layer A evidence; same evidence tier as text/currency.

evidence.layerAEvidence: JTI Document Metadata HTML doc, Class Types section: 'crsReceiptNumber' / 'String. The calendar reservation number generated by the CRS system.' Source file: docs/fileing files/Document Metadata/Document Metadata _ EFM Documentation.html line 626. Same evidence tier as text/currency (HTML doc only, no baseline sample).

existingData.notes: Receipt numbers are scalar identifiers, always new-data.

crsReceiptNumber.promotionCriteria: PROMOTED Step #46. Future promotion to V1 (Tier B live-verified) waits on either: (a) a real court declaring crsReceiptNumber in its policy/UX, OR (b) a baseline sample capturing the wire shape end-to-end. Until then the builder arm + parser arm exist defensively (if a custom policy ever enables this classType, the builder won't NotImplementedException-throw).

number.description: Numeric (decimal) scalar value. **TIER-3 / NOT IN JTI CANONICAL CLASS TYPES LIST** — per Step #46 audit of the JTI Document Metadata HTML doc (`docs/fileing files/Document Metadata/Document Metadata _ EFM Documentation.html`), the canonical 'Class Types' enumeration lists 10 entries (attorney, boolean, caseParticipant, contact, crsReceiptNumber, currency, date, judgment, text, codelist) — `number` is NOT among them. The wrapper element `<numberValue>` exists in the WSDL but JTI does not expose it as a filer-controllable classType. Likely a reserved/server-populated field or extension type. Builder fail-closes via schema-aware default arm.
number.promotionCriteria: Step #46 re-classification: TIER-3 unblock requires EITHER (a) JTI adding `number` to the Document Metadata HTML 'Class Types' enumeration (external doc change), OR (b) an observed sample from any court using `<classType>number</classType>`. Until then, considered structurally out-of-scope rather than awaiting-evidence-blocked.

evidence.layerAEvidence: Plan §T-8 declaration only; candidate scenario CIV-INI-013 (Small Claims Jurisdictional Limit) may exercise it.

wire.niemGrounding: niem-core nc:AmountType. Structured: <nc:Amount currencyCode="USD">123.45</nc:Amount>. Current builder emits decimal as direct leaf — may be wrong, needs Tier A regression once a sample appears.

knownBugs: Builder currently emits `<currencyValue>{decimal}</currencyValue>` directly — suspected wrong per NIEM nc:AmountType structure. Flagged for T-8 cleanup.
currency.promotionCriteria: First sample with classType=currency confirming the nc:Amount wrap.

email.description: Structured email address (beyond contact.email — standalone email-as-metadata). **TIER-3 / NOT IN JTI CANONICAL CLASS TYPES LIST** — per Step #46 audit, JTI HTML Document Metadata 'Class Types' enumerates 10 classTypes and `email` is NOT among them. Email is exposed by JTI via the `contact` classType (nested ContactEmailID) which IS in the canonical list. The standalone `<emailValue>` WSDL wrapper appears to be a reserved field, NOT a filer-controllable classType.
email.promotionCriteria: Step #46 re-classification: TIER-3 unblock requires EITHER (a) JTI adding `email` to the Document Metadata HTML 'Class Types' enumeration, OR (b) an observed sample using `<classType>email</classType>` as a metadata-item classType (NOT the contact.email nested shape, which is already supported). Until then, use the existing `contact` classType for filer email entry.

action.description: Cause-of-action or complaint-line-item within a filing. **TIER-3 / NOT IN JTI CANONICAL CLASS TYPES LIST** — per Step #46 audit, JTI HTML Document Metadata 'Class Types' enumerates 10 classTypes and `action` is NOT among them. The `<actionValue>` wrapper is in the WSDL but JTI does not expose it as a filer-controllable classType. Causes of action are typically captured via case-extension level constructs (ECF 4.0 ecf:ComplaintType) not via metadata items.

wire.niemGrounding: ECF 4.0 ecf:ComplaintType or JTI extension. Complex sub-structure — builder likely deferred behind first-observed-sample gate.

default.fieldsNote: Field list deferred — ComplaintType is complex, needs first sample to determine minimal + optional fields.

action.promotionCriteria: Step #46 re-classification: TIER-3 unblock requires EITHER (a) JTI adding `action` to the Document Metadata HTML 'Class Types' enumeration, OR (b) an observed sample using `<classType>action</classType>` AS A METADATA ITEM (NOT the case-extension ECF 4.0 ecf:ComplaintType, which lives directly on case extensions and isn't a metadata-item classType). Madera GetPolicy probe (Step #46) yielded no classType declarations of any kind.

attorney.description: Attorney-as-a-classType. SEMANTIC synonym of caseAssignment but distinct wire wrapper. JTI HTML docs use this; baseline samples use caseAssignment.

evidence.layerAEvidence: JTI HTML 'Building a Subsequent Filing Payload' example.

wsdl.clrTypeNote: Same CLR type as caseParticipant, different wrapper element. Code MUST dispatch on classType string → wrapper element (§3.0 observation #1). Note both share Order=10/9 adjacency in WSDL.

wire.niemGrounding: Same as caseParticipant — nc:EntityPerson + bar + firm + contact + AssignmentRole.

attorney.description: Alias of caseAssignment.attorney — reuse the same field list.

fields.name: personNameSuffixText
fields.label: Suffix
fields.addedNote: Added 2026-05-17 (Step #12 prep). Was on caseParticipant.person variant (line 66) but missing from caseAssignment.attorney + attorney alias variants — pre-fix the T-4 attorney composite would silently drop suffix even though SF's hand-coded markup emitted `.att-suffix` and the wire model carries CaseAssignmentData.NameSuffix end-to-end (Step #8 closure).

knownBugs: Builder new-data path shares case "caseparticipant": case "attorney" fall-through at ReviewFilingXmlBuilder.cs:832-833; new-data emits <caseParticipantValue>, NOT <attorneyValue>. Fix is T-8 branch split.
attorney.promotionCriteria: First sample OR Madera policy declaring classType=attorney.

address.description: Structured mailing/physical address as a standalone metadata item (distinct from contact.address nested shape). **TIER-3 / NOT IN JTI CANONICAL CLASS TYPES LIST** — per Step #46 audit, JTI HTML Document Metadata 'Class Types' enumerates 10 classTypes and `address` is NOT among them. Addresses are exposed by JTI via the `contact` classType (nested StructuredAddress) which IS in the canonical list. The standalone `<addressValue>` WSDL wrapper is a reserved field, NOT a filer-controllable classType.

wire.niemGrounding: JTI StructuredAddressExt extension — sta1:StructuredAddress. Field set likely mirrors contact.default minus the phone/email bits.

default.description: Reuses address-portion of contact fields. Extract helper from BuildContactInformation per T-8 refactor.
default.auditNote: WSDL field enumeration pending against StructuredAddressType (NIEM-core) at @c:/Users/sevak/workspace/test/src/EFiling/EFiling.WsdlGenerated/FilingReview/Reference.cs:1730 — NIEM type has addressRecipientName, supplementalAddressText, geocoordinates etc. that are deliberately not surfaced for CA-civil filer UX. Per-court promotion possible.

fields.name: state
fields.label: State
fields.sourceCorrectionNote: Was 'STATE' until 2026-05-17. Same fix applied to contact.default (line 161). Live verification against Madera proved STATE returns empty array; US_STATE returns 50 entries.

fields.name: country
fields.label: Country
fields.sourceCorrectionNote: Was 'COUNTRY' until 2026-05-17. Same fix applied to contact.default (line 163). ADDRESS_COUNTRY is the canonical JTI name (per memory 8fc21aaf) and returns 4814 vs 797 bytes on Madera.

address.promotionCriteria: Step #46 re-classification: TIER-3 unblock requires EITHER (a) JTI adding `address` to the Document Metadata HTML 'Class Types' enumeration, OR (b) an observed sample using `<classType>address</classType>` as a metadata-item classType (NOT contact.address). Until then, use the existing `contact` / `caseParticipant` classTypes for filer address entry.

document.description: Document-reference-as-metadata (NOT the document being filed — that's coreFilingMessage/DocumentSubmission). **TIER-3 / NOT IN JTI CANONICAL CLASS TYPES LIST** — per Step #46 audit, JTI HTML Document Metadata 'Class Types' enumerates 10 classTypes and `document` is NOT among them. The `<documentValue>` wrapper is in the WSDL but JTI does not expose it as a filer-controllable classType. Likely a server-populated cross-reference field.

wire.niemGrounding: JTI CourtEventDocumentTypeExt extension. Represents a cross-reference to a document associated with a court event.

default.fieldsNote: Field list deferred — CourtEventDocumentTypeExt is complex. Fail-closed until sample surfaces.

document.promotionCriteria: Step #46 re-classification: TIER-3 unblock requires EITHER (a) JTI adding `document` to the Document Metadata HTML 'Class Types' enumeration, OR (b) an observed sample using `<classType>document</classType>` AS A METADATA ITEM. Note: Madera GetPolicy probe (Step #46 Path 1) returned NO classType enumeration — the policy structure only enumerates CourtCodelist URIs (CASE_CATEGORY, ADDRESS_TYPE, etc.) and three ECFElementName entries (AttorneyValue, DocumentIdentification → documentList, CourtName → zipCode). `DocumentIdentification` maps to a document-list source for picker UI, NOT to the `document` classType.

scheduledEvent.description: Hearing / trial / calendared event reference. **TIER-3 / NOT IN JTI CANONICAL CLASS TYPES LIST** — per Step #46 audit, JTI HTML Document Metadata 'Class Types' enumerates 10 classTypes and `scheduledEvent` is NOT among them. The `<scheduledEventValue>` wrapper is in the WSDL but JTI does not expose it as a filer-controllable classType.

default.fieldsNote: Field list deferred — CourtEventTypeExt is complex.

scheduledEvent.promotionCriteria: Step #46 re-classification: TIER-3 unblock requires EITHER (a) JTI adding `scheduledEvent` to the Document Metadata HTML 'Class Types' enumeration, OR (b) an observed sample using `<classType>scheduledEvent</classType>`. The Madera GetPolicy probe (Step #46) lists EVENT_TYPE as a CourtCodelist but that's a code-list of event categories, not a classType declaration.

caseSpecialStatus.description: Per-case special status flag (scalar string). **TIER-3 / NOT IN JTI CANONICAL CLASS TYPES LIST** — per Step #46 audit, JTI HTML Document Metadata 'Class Types' enumerates 10 classTypes and `caseSpecialStatus` (the metadata-item classType) is NOT among them. **Important distinction:** the LASC `<ns6:CaseSpecialStatus>` at CASE-extension level (e.g., UDCOV19 indicator, documented in Subsequent Filing Court Specific Concepts HTML) is a SEPARATE wire shape from the metadata-item `<caseSpecialStatusValue>` wrapper. The case-level construct IS documented and IS used for LASC COVID-19 stipulations + Bankruptcy indicators; the metadata-item classType is NOT documented and presumably reserved.
caseSpecialStatus.promotionCriteria: Step #46 re-classification: TIER-3 unblock requires EITHER (a) JTI adding `caseSpecialStatus` (the metadata-item form) to the Document Metadata HTML 'Class Types' enumeration, OR (b) an observed sample using `<classType>caseSpecialStatus</classType>` AS A METADATA ITEM (NOT the CASE-extension level `<CaseSpecialStatus>` already documented in catalog §5.6.3 — that's a different wire shape served by direct CivilCaseTypeExt emission, not via documentFilingMetaDataItem).

judgment.description: Judgment reference (existing-data: pointer to a pre-existing judgment on the case by id). WSDL wrapper field is PLURAL: <judgments>, not <judgmentValue>. Content element <judgmentId> lives in a DIFFERENT namespace (CourtEventJudgment) than the wrapper (DocumentFilingMetaData) — judgment is the only classType where wrapper + content namespaces split.

evidence.layerAEvidence: LASC vendor sample 'Example Filing a Writ of Return Sample.xml' (existing-data with <ns10:judgmentId>2562247</ns10:judgmentId>). Note: catalog §3.19 previously cited the Alameda 'Abstract of Judgment - Civil and Small Claims Sample.xml' as Layer B evidence — that citation is misleading; the Alameda sample contains zero judgment metadata. Step #15 audit corrected the citation. Stays V2 (Layer A vendor only) until a canonical SF baseline scenario is captured.

wsdl.wireElementNote: PLURAL — field name is `judgments`, wire element is `<judgments>`. Not `<judgmentValue>`. Content element `<judgmentId>` is in namespace `urn:com.journaltech:ecourt:ecf:extension:CourtEventJudgment` per CourtEventJudgmentType (FilingReview/Reference.cs:22743).

existingData.wireNote: Multiple <ns10:judgmentId> children may appear inside one <ns9:judgments> wrapper (WSDL declares CourtEventJudgmentType[] — array). Observed sample only shows 1, but multi-id is wire-spec valid.
existingData.evidence: LASC Writ of Return Sample (Layer A vendor doc, 2020-03-20).

newData.wireNote: WSDL hosts JudgmentAwardType (FilingReview/Reference.cs:22832) + JudgmentAwardPartyType (FilingReview/Reference.cs:22872) for filing a brand-new judgment with creditor/debtor party refs + amount + date. NO observed sample. Builder throws NotImplementedException for new-data path.

fields.name: judgmentId
fields.label: Judgment
fields.fieldNote: Existing-data only. Picker source populated by CaseResponseParser.ParseJudgments (Step #48, 2026-05-21) from <CaseCourtEvent xsi:type=CourtEventJudgmentType> elements in the GetCase response. SF.cshtml's `buildJudgmentSection` renders a <select> from the `caseJudgments` JS array. Empty case → disabled select + explanation message.

judgment.promotionCriteria: Step #48 — follow-up (1) DONE: frontend caseJudgments loader landed via server-side eager projection. CaseInfo.Judgments populated by CaseResponseParser.ParseJudgments (extracts <CaseCourtEvent xsi:type=CourtEventJudgmentType> using structural discriminator 'has judgmentId child'). SF.cshtml serializes Model.Judgments to a caseJudgments JS array (mirrors caseParties pattern) and the judgment dispatcher arm renders a <select> populated from it. 6 Step48 parser tests lock the LASC HTML wire shape + multi-judgment + empty-state + discriminator (non-judgment CaseCourtEvent ignored) + empty-id-skip + dedupe invariants. **Remaining follow-up (2):** Capture a new-data baseline sample exercising JudgmentAwardType + JudgmentAwardPartyType to flip newData.awaitingEvidence=false — still blocked on baseline sample availability.

relatedCase.description: Cross-reference to another case (related-case designation, consolidation, transfer). **TIER-3 / NOT IN JTI CANONICAL CLASS TYPES LIST** — per Step #46 audit, JTI HTML Document Metadata 'Class Types' enumerates 10 classTypes and `relatedCase` is NOT among them. The `<relatedCaseValue>` wrapper is in the WSDL but JTI does not expose it as a filer-controllable classType.
relatedCase.promotionCriteria: Step #46 re-classification: TIER-3 unblock requires EITHER (a) JTI adding `relatedCase` to the Document Metadata HTML 'Class Types' enumeration, OR (b) an observed sample using `<classType>relatedCase</classType>`. Note: related-case associations DO exist in the WSDL at the case-extension level (RelatedCaseAssociationType in CivilCaseTypeExt) which is a different wire shape from the metadata-item `<relatedCaseValue>` wrapper.
